name: Chang'e 1
description: China's first lunar mission, 2007-2009. Built and operated as a deliberate precursor test for the Chang'e 3 lander — the orbital phase served as a flight-qualification rehearsal for the deep-space communications and orbital mechanics that the lander mission would depend on. Crashed into the Moon at end of life as a controlled impact test.
scale_fact: Lunar polar orbit at ~200 km altitude. First Chinese lunar mission; opened the Chang'e programme that delivered the only foreign sample-return missions of the 21st century.
